column2: 你已經砸過窗戶了。為什麼要再砸？

column1: You might try reaching over to the window first.
column2: 你可以先試試伸手去那扇窗。

column1: You could never pry that window open with your pocket knife.
column2: 你用小刀根本撬不開那扇窗。

column1: That's an idea; it's just not a good idea!
column2: 這是個主意；就是不是個好主意！

column1: The fence is much too tall for you to climb over.
column2: 柵欄對你來說太高了，爬不過去。

column1: Unfortunately the lid on the trash bin just will not close.
column2: 可惜垃圾桶的蓋子就是蓋不上。

column1: Smart move, Larry!
column2: 聰明的舉動，Larry！

column1: A sense of foreboding washes over your heart when your eyes slowly examine this dark alley that seems to be just outside every public area in Lost Wages. Is that just another kindly citizen or a vicious mugger quickly approaching you?
column2: 當你的眼神慢慢掃過這暗巷——似乎在失城每個公共區域旁邊都有一條—— 不詳的預感籠罩著你的心。朝你快步走來的是善良市民還是兇狠歹徒？

column1: Larry, when are you gonna learn to stay out of these dark alleys!!
column2: Larry，你何時才要學會別進這些暗巷啦！！

column1: Hey, guys!! He's screwed up again! Dump this one down the tubes, and get him another body!
column2: 嘿，各位！他又搞砸了！把這個丟進下水道，給他換一具身體！

column1: Pardon me, buddy, you say, "do you have any spare change?"
column2: 麻煩一下，老兄，你說，"有零錢嗎？"

column1: (He seems unamused!)
column2: (他看起來不爽！)

column1: Hey, you! you challenge, "come over here and fight like a man!"
column2: 嘿，你！你挑釁道，"過來像個男人一樣跟我幹！"

column1: (Dumb idea, Larry!)
column2: (爛主意，Larry！)

column1: Your attempt to frighten the mugger fails.
column2: 你嚇唬搶匪的企圖失敗了。

column1: There's only one thing he wants, Larry: everything!
column2: 他就要一樣東西，Larry：你的全部！

column1: (Now you know what we have to go through every time you press "Restart!")
column2: (現在你知道每次按『重新開始』我們要經歷什麼了！)

column1: You wonder if cab companies have full-time employees whose sole job is to make these floors sticky.
column2: 你懷疑計程車公司有沒有專職員工做的工作就是讓車廂地板黏黏的。

column1: Sorry, Mac, but my meter's runnin'. Where didja wanna go?
column2: 抱歉啦老兄，我的錶一直在跑。你要去哪？

column1: Waddaya tryin' to do, peahead! You're not gonna stiff me, and get away with it! Step outside, Deadbeat!!
column2: 你他媽想幹嘛，豌豆腦！別想賴帳就脫身！滾下車，欠債仔！！

column1: Whew! You're glad to get out of here.
column2: 呼！你很高興終於逃出來了。

column1: So, wadda we gonna do, buddy? Ya just gonna sit here all night? Some guys gotta earn a livin', ya know!
column2: 那怎樣，老兄？你就想在這兒坐一整晚？有人得賺生活費，你知道吧！

column1: So? Ya gonna pay up? Or does I have ta get rough wit' cha??
column2: 那咧？你要付錢？還是我得動粗？？

column1: So? Ya gonna get outta my cab or not? Thanks for da business, but I didn't want ta make no career outta this!
column2: 所以？你要滾下車還是不？感謝你的生意，但我不想以此為業啦！

column1: The cabbie snarls a "Where to, buddy?" as he starts the meter rolling.
column2: 計程車司機邊發動錶邊咆哮"要去哪，老兄？"

column1: Well, here we are, buddy. That'll be $%d.00.
column2: 好了，到地方了，老兄。共 $%d.00。

column1: Thanks, buddy. Maybe I'll see ya again sometime!
column2: 「謝了，老兄。也許哪天還會見面！」

column1: The driver looks at you and says, "Hey! What's that you got in your coat there? Is that wine?"
column2: 司機瞥著你說，"嘿！你外套裡那是什麼？紅酒？"

column1: Before you can move, the cabbie grabs your precious box of wine and guzzles it all down! Ohmigawd, Larry, hang on!!
column2: 還沒等你反應，計程車司機搶過你珍貴的紅酒盒一飲而盡！天啊，Larry，抓緊啦！！

column1: Brace yourself, Larry. He's swerving all over the road!
column2: 用力抱著，Larry。他在路上亂竄！

column1: It seems a bridge abutment has brought your evening in Lost Wages to a sudden, final, and complete halt!
column2: 橋墩似乎把你在失城的夜晚來了一個突然、終局、完全的停止！

column1: No air bag, eh?
column2: 沒有氣囊，嗯？

column1: Remember, Larry: "Friends don't let cabbies drive drunk!"
column2: 記著，Larry："朋友不會讓計程車司機酒駕！"

column1: You wish you hadn't. Mr. America this guy ain't. His strongest feature is his aroma!
column2: 你後悔了。這傢伙根本不是美國佬。他最突出的特色是他的臭氣！

column1: The cabbie wonders why you keep looking at him, but in the mirror he flashes you a coy little smile and a knowing wink.
column2: 計程車司機納悶你為什麼老看他，但他從鏡子裡對你一個嬌嗔的微笑和賊兮兮的眨眼。

column1: (Your stomach turns, and not from the ride!)
column2: (你胃一翻，不是因為坐車！)

column1: You can't reach the cabbie from your seat. Why not talk to him?
column2: 你坐著夠不著司機。何不跟他聊天？

column1: Yuck! Your stomach turns at the thought.
column2: 呃！一想到就胃痛。

column1: This cab smells terrible!
column2: 這台車臭死人！

column1: We're there already; just sit tight, willya, Buddy?
column2: 已經到了；坐著別動，好嗎，老兄？

column1: You ask the cabbie, "Where's the action?"
column2: 你問司機，"哪兒有樂子？"

column1: Lookin' for action, eh? Dis town is really full of it. We gotta disco full of foxes this time of night. Den dere's the casino, next to the all-night weddin' chapel. And we gotta lovely bar, but you've already been dere. Me, I like da convenience store next to da disco, but of course, that's just personal preference.
column2: 找樂子，嗯？這鎮子真是多得是。這時候舞廳滿是辣妹。然後還有賭場，在全夜禮拜堂旁。還有間酒吧，不過你已經去過了。我啊，就愛舞廳旁的便利超商，當然，那只是我的喜好。

column1: Ok. You got it, Mac!
column2: 「好啦，沒問題，老兄！」

column1: It won't take us long to get back dere, but I'll have to circle 'round da block.
column2: 「回那邊不會花太久，不過我得繞過街區。」

column1: Uh, excuse me, Mr. Driver, you say, "but I think I may wish to change my mind about my destination this evening. Would that be all right with you?"
column2: 呃，司機先生，你說，"我想我可能要改變今晚的目的地。可以嗎？"

column1: Geez, some guys jes' can't make up der minds!
column2: 天啊，有些人就是改不了主意！

column1: Uh, excuse me, Mr. Driver, you say, "but I think I may wish to change my mind yet again about my destination this evening. Would that be too much trouble?"
column2: 呃，司機先生，你說，"我想我又要再改一次今晚的目的地。太麻煩了嗎？"

column1: Nah, ya changed your min' already! I ain' gonna spen' all night driving you in circles! Next time, figgur out where ya wanna go before ya gets in da cab!
column2: 不行，你已經改過了！我不會一整晚載著你轉圈圈！下次上車前先想好要去哪！

column1: Sheepishly, you apologize to the cabbie for not carrying enough money to pay for the ride.
column2: 你尷尬地向計程車司機道歉，因為沒帶足夠的錢付車資。

column1: Waddaya mean, `no money!!' snarls the driver. "Step outsid'a ma office, chump!"
column2: 你說什麼『沒錢！！』司機咆哮。"滾出我的地盤，白癡！"

column1: I appreciate your offer of a tip, but it's against my personal philosophy to accept gratuities!
column2: 我感謝你的小費，但收小費違反我的人生哲學！

column1: Why don'cha jes' wait'll we git there, buddy?
column2: 你為什麼不等到下車，老兄？

column1: You quietly and carefully spritz a little spray around, vainly trying to kill some of this odor!
column2: 你輕輕噴了點香水，徒勞地想壓制這臭味！

column1: Nah, fruit gives me gas! he grunts.
column2: 不行，水果讓我放屁！他咕噥著。

column1: Hey, hurry up, you cry, "can't you see it's getting late?!"
column2: 嘿，快點，你喊道，"沒看天色晚了嗎？！"

column1: Time waits for no man! he replies cryptically.
column2: 時光不等人！他神祕兮兮地說。

column1: I got no use for a diamond ring, shouts the cabbie, "especially one wit a stone like dat! Dat sucker sure looks fake to me!"
column2: 鑽戒對我沒用，計程車司機喊道，"特別是那塊石頭！那東西看起來超假！"

column1: No, thanks, buddy, replies the cabbie, "but have you got any wine?"
column2: 不用，老兄，司機回道，"不過你有紅酒嗎？"

column1: What's that, a tricorder? he asks. "Don't tell me you're one of dem science fiction freaks?"
column2: 那是什麼，萬用儀？他問。"別說你是那種科幻迷？"

column1: Well, ain't chu all growed up!
column2: 嘿，你可真長大了！

column1: Thanks, buddy, says the cabbie, as he grabs your box of candy and proceeds to gobble down every piece!
column2: 謝啦，老兄，司機說著搶過你的糖果盒狂吞猛嚥！

column1: (Bad move, Larry! You feel certain you're going to meet someone someday who would really enjoy a piece!)
column2: (慘了，Larry！你肯定哪天會碰到一個正妹真的很想咬一口！)

column1: No, I don't want to go dancin' with you later, says the cabbie.
column2: 「免了，」計程車司機說，「我不要跟你等等去跳舞。」

column1: Not HIM again! Geez, does that guy get around. And I DO mean around!
column2: 「又是那傢伙！老天爺，這哥兒們跟女人的關係真不少。我說的是那種關係！」

column1: The air in here IS thick enough to cut!
column2: 這裡的色氣濃得能用刀子切！

column1: No, thanks, says the cabbie, "I gotta subscription!"
column2: 「免了，」司機說，「老子有固定的！」

column1: Don't hit the cabbie's head with your hammer; you might hurt it.
column2: 別用槌子敲司機的頭，你可能會傷著他。

column1: (The hammer, that is!)
column2: (槌子啦，蠢蛋！)

column1: Hey, thanks! he cries. "One of those and I'll be stuck in fifth gear all night."
column2: 「嘿，謝了！」他大喊。「來一粒我整晚都會卡在五檔！」

column1: (Good idea, Larry! After all you had to go through to get those pills!)
column2: (想得好，Larry！你費了那麼大工夫才弄到這些藥丸！)

column1: Wat a coinci-dince! I wuz jes talkin' 'bout ribbon like dat with dis stacked babe fare I hadda little while ago. She wuz tellin' me 'bout how she tied up some dumb dweeb and took all his dough. Boy, did we have a good laugh at that one! You shouldda been here!!
column2: 「啊呀！超巧合！我才剛跟一個身材火辣的妹妹說到這種繩子。她跟我說她怎麼綁住某個白癡然後搜括他的全部家產。天啊，我們笑到不行！你應該要在這裡的！」

column1: (Somehow you feel you were!)
column2: (不知為何，你覺得自己有在場！)

column1: Here, take this! you offer.
column2: 「給你，拿去！」你說。

column1: Nah, I got no need for that! he replies.
column2: 「不用，」他回答，「我用不著那鬼東西！」

column1: You scratch your head in amazement at the way this cab can still operate with such severe rear wheel camber!
column2: 你搔頭驚嘆這輛計程車後輪傾斜成那樣還能動彈。

column1: Talkin' to yourself, eh? smirks the cabbie. "A guy in a job like this runs into lots o' weirdos!"
column2: 「跟自己說話，嗯？」司機怪笑。「幹這行的哥兒們什麼怪咖都見過！」

column1: Leave yourself alone, Larry!
column2: 放過自己吧，Larry！

column1: It seems to be running a little fast, but then, this is an expensive town!
column2: 好像跑得有點快，不過嘛，這是個高消費的城鎮！

column1: You don't really want to steal his license plate!
column2: 你其實不想偷他的車牌啦！

column2: 你沒有後車廂的鑰匙。

column2: 電子二十一點機上方的數字顯示你目前的下注金額和機器裡的總餘額。

column2: 「滾開，爛機器！」你大罵，你可不會被機械嚇到。

column2: 你輕輕親了親機器祈求好運。

column1: Good idea! You smash the video blackjack machine with your hammer, causing hundreds of dollars to spill out, which you gladly catch and put in your pockets.
column2: 絕妙主意！你拿槌子砸爛電子二十一點機，成百上千的鈔票掉出來，你喜孜孜地接住塞進口袋。

column1: Bad idea! The security guard handles you indelicately as he escorts you to the local jail!
column2: 爛透了！保安用不太溫柔的方式把你押去監獄！

column1: ...maybe with good behavior?

column2: Larry，下次溫柔一點對待機器啦！

column2: 你可以這樣玩整晚！

column2: 請下注偶數美金（2、4、6等等）。這台機器不付零頭。

column1: This machine has a $10.00 minimum bet and a $10,000.00 limit.  Bets must be a multiple of $2.00.  Dealer must hit 16, and must stand on 17, unless he has an ace.  Blackjack pays 3 to 2 odds.  Any six cards under 22 wins.
column2: 這台機器最少下注$10.00，上限$10,000.00。下注必須是$2.00的倍數。莊家必須補牌到16點，17點必須停牌，除非他有Ace。二十一點付3比2賠率。任何六張牌加起來不超過二十二點就贏。

column1: Insurance bets are offered whenever the dealer shows an ace. They are half your bet, and pay 2 to 1 odds.  You may split a pair or surrender whenever you have only two cards.  You may double down if your cards total 10 or 11 points.
column2: 莊家有Ace時會提供保險下注。保險下注是你的下注的一半，付2比1賠率。任何時候只要你只有兩張牌就可以分牌或放棄。如果你的牌加起來是十或十一點就可以加倍。

column1: Uh, gosh, Fawn, you mumble. "I've only got $%d.00 bucks on me!"
column2: 「呃，天啊，Fawn，」你嗯嗯嗖嗖地說，「我身上只有 $%d.00 啦！」

column1: I'm sorry, but I'll need at least two hundred dollars, she says with a coy smile. "You know it'll be worth it!"
column2: 「抱歉，但我至少需要兩百塊，」她嬌羞地微笑著說，「你知道會值得的！」

column1: It appears Fawn has caught you a "little short," Larry. Better try the casino, and hope for a run of good luck!
column2: 看起來佛恩把你的錢看得很緊，拉瑞。最好去賭場碰碰運氣！

column1: You fervently hope she'll wait for your return.
column2: 你衷心希望她會等你回來。

column1: Conveniently, she offers, "I'll wait right here until you return."
column2: 方便的是，她說，「我就在這兒等你回來。」

column1: Gee, Larry, this is working out swell.
column2: 天哪，拉瑞，這進展真是太棒了。

column1: Ooh you're so sweet. I just love money.
column2: 「哦你真貼心。我就是喜歡鈔票。」

column1: Thanks, Larry. You won't regret this. Meet me at the Marriage Chapel near the Casino in a few minutes. I'll get us a room. Hurry, Larry, I can hardly wait!
column2: 「謝謝你，拉瑞。你不會後悔的。幾分鐘後在賭場附近的結婚禮堂見我。我會訂房。快點，拉瑞，我等不及了！」

column1: Ooh you're so sweet. I just love roses.
column2: 「哦你真貼心。我就是喜歡玫瑰花。」

column1: Ooh you're so sweet. I just love Candy.
column2: 「哦你真貼心。我就是喜歡糖果。」

column1: Ooh you're so sweet. I just love diamonds.
column2: 「哦你真貼心。我就是喜歡鑽石。」

column1: Are you implying I have bad breath? she says angrily.
column2: 「你是在暗示我口氣不好嗎？」她生氣地說。

column1: It IS getting late, Larry. And you're sure she's soooo lonely!
column2: 時間確實不早了，Larry。你確信她超級寂寞！

column1: Why, Larry! What a cute little clicker you've got.
column2: 「哇，Larry！你拿著那麼可愛的小遙控器。」

column1: You're rushing things, Larry. Calm down.
column2: 你太急了，Larry。冷靜點。

column1: She'd probably dance with you if you'd offer your hand.
column2: 如果你主動伸手，她大概會跟你跳舞。

column1: It would be smart for you not to let Fawn know you're carrying a concealed weapon!
column2: 最聰明的做法就是別讓 Fawn 知道你藏著武器！

column1: I have no interest in naked women--or window washing! Fawn says.
column2: 「我對裸女沒興趣——也不想洗窗戶！」Fawn 說。

column1: Oooh, I just LOVE to play with manly tools!
column2: 「哦哦，我最愛玩大丈夫的工具了！」

column1: Ken? Never heard of him, she says. Then her expression changes. "Say... what's he like? How much does he make? Is he married?"
column2: 「Ken？沒聽過，」她說。然後表情一變，「等等……他長什麼樣？他賺多少？他結婚了嗎？」

column1: Oh, %s is sweet, but I'd like something special... from a guy like you!
column2: 「哦，%s 很可愛，不過我想要點特別的……來自你這樣的傢伙！」

column1: Her mind is stuck in the present(s).
column2: 她的心思只在禮物上。

column1: A long and lovely neck perfectly fills the distance between her head and her shoulders!
column2: 一條長長的、迷人的脖子完美地填滿了她頭部和肩膀之間的距離！

column1: Ooooh, that feels nice!
column2: 「哦哦哦，感覺好舒服！」

column1: Oh, no! What's that? In the east! It's... it's... it's the sun!
column2: 不對勁！那是什麼？在東方！是……是……是太陽！

column1: Glancing at your watch, you realize the terrible truth. Your night in Lost Wages is over, and (technically speaking) you're still...
column2: 瞥了一眼手錶，你意識到一個可怕的事實。你在迷城的夜晚結束了，而且（嚴格來說）你還是……

column1: VIRGIN!!!
column2: 處男！！！

column1: Larry-cakes anyone?
column2: 誰要 Larry 蛋糕？

column1: Life is no longer worth living!
column2: 人生再也沒意思了！

column1: You'll never ride with me again!
column2: 「你再也別想坐我的車了！」

column1: It's never a good idea to "stiff a cabbie!"
column2: 「欺騙計程車司機」絕不是好主意！

column1: Stop whistling and get in the cab!
column2: 「別吹口哨了，上車！」

column1: Too late now, Ace-(in-the)-hole! shouts the rude cabbie, "I'm outta here!"
column2: 「太晚了，笨蛋！」粗魯的計程車司機大喊，「我要閃人了！」

column1: Hold your horses, he's on his way!
column2: 別急，他正在來的路上！

column1: I ain' gonna wait all day, bub! yells the cabbie.
column2: 「我不會一整天都在等，老兄！」計程車司機吼道。

column1: That's you all over!
column2: 這根本就是你啊！

column1: Larry, the traffic here is really a killer! You should forget about crossing these streets!
column2: Larry，這邊的交通真的要人命！你應該放棄橫越街道的念頭！

column1: It's not very clean.
column2: 它不太乾淨。

column1: It's not very safe.
column2: 這不太安全。

column1: (And those are its good points!)
column2: （這還是它的優點呢！）

column1: It smells like an old cab.
column2: 聞起來像老計程車。

column1: It's too late now, Larry!
column2: 太晚了，Larry！

column1: Vowing to hold your breath forever, you enter this miserable excuse for public transportation.
column2: 你發誓要忍著呼吸，\n踏進這台破得不像話的大眾運輸。

column1: Obviously, he thinks you're a fire plug.
column2: 顯然他把你當成消防栓。

column1: You hope he's soon a "Hmong" friends.
column2: 你希望他很快就成為「老友」。

column1: Dogs like that make you want to keep moving.

column1: As you bend over to show that dog who's boss, his bare teeth convince you there are worse things than damp trousers!

column1: The dog smells quite bad, although still better than you.

column1: It would seem turn-about would be fair play, but you just don't have it in you!
column2: 雖然輪流虧待似乎很公平，但你根本做不出來！

column2: 你玩了 %d 分鐘 %d 秒就才賺 %d 點？！

column2: 你玩了 %d 分鐘 %d 秒一分都沒賺？

column1: Get a life!
column2: 去過生活吧！

column1: Click the OK button to close this window.
column2: 按 OK 按鈕關掉這個視窗。

column1: Your wallet is mostly empty since you stuck all your money into this machine!
column2: 你的錢包空蕩蕩的，因為你把所有的錢都塞進這台機器了！

column1: Your wallet contains some wrinkled business cards, lots of notes, some credit cards (which won't work in this game), and $%d.00 in cash.
column2: 你的錢包裡有一些皺巴巴的名片、一堆紙條、幾張信用卡（在遊戲裡沒用），和 $%d.00 現金。

column1: You reach in your wallet and grab the...
column2: 你掏出錢包抓了…

column1: What a shame! Not one single business in Lost Wages accepts credit cards from the First National Bank of Iraq.
column2: 真丟人！Lost Wages 沒有任何生意接受伊拉克第一銀行的信用卡。

column1: The only business card you have reads, "Sierra On-Line, Inc. (209) 683-8989"
column2: 你唯一的名片上寫著「Sierra On-Line, Inc. (209) 683-8989」

column1: Remember to buy more Al Lowe games!
column2: 「記得買更多艾爾·洛的遊戲！」

column1: Gee, a genuine BowlX!  At least the time is accurate -- it's %02d:%02d.
column2: 天啊，真正的 BowlX！至少時間對——現在是 %02d:%02d。

column1: You press the button on the remote control and...
column2: 你按下遙控器的按鈕，然後...

column1: ...nothing happens.
column2: ...什麼都沒發生。

column1: The passcard reads:  "Lost Wages Disco  Admit One Member and Guest  Remember: every Monday is Ladies Night!"
column2: 通行證上寫著：「Lost Wages Disco  一位會員和嘉賓通行  記住：每個星期一是女士之夜！」

column1: Well, well. It looks like this month's issue is filled with intelligent, literary articles laden with redeeming social value. For example, here's an article about how professional window washers secure themselves to the sides of tall buildings with a rope tied around their waist...
column2: 嗯呃，這月號看起來全是有內涵又有文采的文章，滿是社會價值。舉例來說，有篇文章講專業洗窗工怎麼用繩子綁腰部固定在大樓外牆上…

column1: Nah! Who wants to read that crap? You quickly flip through the pages until you discover...
column2: 呸！誰想讀那爛玩意？你快速翻著頁面直到發現…

column1: Whoaaah! Nice centerfold this month!!
column2: 哇嗚！這月的中頁好棒！！

column1: error!
column2: 錯誤！

column1: No need to pay the breath spray, it's just doing its job.
column2: 不用付那個除臭噴劑的錢，它只是在做工作。

column1: The watch doesn't need your money.
column2: 手錶不需要你的錢。

column1: You've already paid for the apple once--and a princely sum, at that.
column2: 你已經付過那個蘋果的錢了——而且是筆鉅款。

column1: You show your genuine vinyl-style wallet to the ring, but it doesn't respond.
column2: 你把真皮人造皮錢包秀給戒指瞧，但它沒半點反應。

column1: Seeking that "leatherette" taste, you foolishly dip your wallet in the whiskey.
column2: 為了嚐那「人造皮」的味道，你蠢蛋似地把錢包泡進威士忌。

column1: The remote control has no use for your wallet.
column2: 遙控器對你的錢包沒轍。

column1: You offer to pay Rose, but it humbly declines.
column2: 你提議用錢包打點玫瑰，但它謙虛地拒絕了。

column1: No, no, no! That's not what lubbers are for!
column2: 不行不行不行！房間不是拿來這樣用的啦！

column1: Your wallet won't fit in the candy box.
column2: 你的錢包塞不進糖果盒。

column1: The disco pass is already paid for.
column2: 迪斯可通行證已經付過錢了。

column1: The pocket knife seems unimpressed with your wallet.
column2: 小刀對你的錢包不感興趣。

column1: You foolishly dip your wallet into the wine.
column2: 你蠢蛋似地把錢包泡進葡萄酒裡。

column1: The girls in the magazine are mightily impressed with your manly, bulging wallet.
column2: 雜誌上的女孩們被你那鼓鼓的男人味錢包迷得不行。

column1: There's no need to put the wallet on the hammer.
column2: 沒必要把錢包放在錘子上。

column1: Try as you might, you can't stuff the wallet into the pill container.
column2: 再怎麼努力，你也塞不進這藥瓶。

column1: You show the wallet to the ribbon, but (strange but true) it doesn't respond.
column2: 你把錢包秀給絲帶瞧，但（奇怪但真的）它沒反應。

column1: You place the graffiti in your wallet (but it still shows up here!).
column2: 你把塗鴉放進錢包（但它還是會出現在這裡！）。

column1: Don't waste precious breath spray on your wallet.
column2: 別浪費珍貴的口氣噴霧在錢包上。

column1: Yep. It's your breath spray.
column2: 沒錯。這是你的口氣噴霧。

column1: A little breath spray makes even the cheapest watch shine like gold (plate).
column2: 一點口氣噴霧就能讓最廉價的手錶閃閃生光（鍍金）。

column1: The apple was previously well-sprayed with an assortment of interesting chemical compounds.
column2: 蘋果之前已經被各種有趣的化學物質噴得透徹。

column1: That ought to kill the taste of Lefty's sink!
column2: 那應該能掩蓋左仔水槽的臭味！

column1: No amount of breath spray will help the taste of this stuff.
column2: 再多的口氣噴霧也救不了這東西的味道。

column1: The remote control's keys are sticky enough already.
column2: 遙控器的按鈕已經夠黏的了。

column1: The rose smells good enough already.
column2: 玫瑰花已經夠香的了。

column1: Your breath spray will clash with that natural minty taste of latex.
column2: 你的口氣噴霧會跟那天然薄膜薄荷味起衝突。

column1: The candy smells great just the way it is.
column2: 糖果本身的香味已經棒了。

column1: Perhaps you should use the breath spray AT the disco instead!
column2: 也許你應該在迪斯可現場才用口氣噴霧！

column1: It's pointless to freshen your knife's breath, let alone its width!
column2: 用口氣噴霧給小刀「清新」完全沒意義，更別提它的寬度了！

column1: Mmm, Chablis with a hint of wintergreen! You may have something there.
column2: 嗯，夏布利加點冬綠香氣！你這想法不賴。

column1: Better not, the pages are sticky enough already.
column2: 別這樣，紙頁已經夠黏的了。

column1: Now the hammer is minty fresh.
column2: 現在錘子聞起來有薄荷香。

column1: That would ruin the wonderful medicinal flavor of the pills.
column2: 那會毀掉藥丸那美妙的藥味。

column1: Yeah, great idea.
column2: 是啊，棒透了。

column1: You'd better not; you don't want to smear your writing.
column2: 你最好別這樣；你不想把自己的字跡弄髒。

column1: Your watch won't fit in your already bulging wallet.
column2: 你的手錶塞不進你已經滿脹的錢包。

column1: Your breath spray couldn't care less what time it is.
column2: 你的口氣噴霧根本不在乎現在幾點。

column1: Pretty clever! "Watch the apple!!"
column2: 真聰明！"看著蘋果！"

column1: The expensive ring, seeing your cheap watch, exudes a strong sense of superiority.
column2: 那貴重的戒指看到你的廉價手錶，散發出強烈的優越感。

column1: You dip your watch in the whiskey, then give it a licking, yet it keeps on ticking!
column2: 你把手錶浸進威士忌，然後舔一舔，但它還是一直滴答滴答。

column1: Is there something good to "watch" on TV, Larry?
column2: 電視上有什麼值得「看」的東西嗎，Larry？

column1: So you want to time the rose?
column2: 所以你想用手錶給玫瑰計時？

column1: That should make your cheap watch waterproof!
column2: 那應該能防水你那便宜手錶！

column1: Hiding your watch in the box of candy won't get you anywhere.
column2: 把手錶藏在糖果盒裡也到不了哪去。

column1: The pass couldn't be less interested.
column2: 通行證一點興趣都沒有。

column1: To a pocketknife, time is relative.
column2: 對小刀來說，時間是相對的。

column1: You idiotically dip your watch into the wine. How much punishment can these things take?
column2: 你蠢蛋似地把手錶泡進葡萄酒。這東西能承受多少折騰？

column1: Almost time to buy the next issue!
column2: 差不多該買下一期了！

column1: The hammer doesn't want to watch.
column2: 錘子不想看。

column1: The watch won't fit into the little pill container.
column2: 手錶塞不進小藥瓶。

column1: You attempt to measure the length of the ribbon with your watch, and find it's about 30 seconds long!
column2: 你用手錶試著測量絲帶長度，發現大約 30 秒長！

column1: What a stupid thing to try.
column2: 真是蠢到爆的主意。

column1: The apple won't fit in your already-bulging wallet.
column2: 蘋果塞不進你已經滿脹的錢包。

column1: Warning: do not try to puncture the breath spray with an apple.
column2: 警告：別試圖用蘋果刺穿口氣噴霧。

column1: The watch concentrates on keeping time and ignores your apple.
column2: 手錶專注於計時，忽視你的蘋果。

column1: This Red Delicious is not suitable for your Granny Smith, but someone else might find it appropriate.
column2: 這紅蘋果不適合你的青蘋果，但別人可能覺得挺搭的。

column1: No, the apple doesn't fit through the ring.
column2: 不行，蘋果穿不過戒指。

column1: You fruitlessly attempt to wedge the apple into the whiskey glass.
column2: 你徒勞地試圖把蘋果塞進威士忌杯。

column1: Having no infrared circuitry, the apple fails to interface with the remote control.
column2: 蘋果沒紅外線電路，沒辦法跟遙控器相容。

column1: The apple and the rose, two of nature's most perfect creations. So what?
column2: 蘋果和玫瑰，大自然最完美的兩個造物。那又怎樣？

column1: The apple already has a protective skin. Don't bite either one!
column2: 蘋果已經有保護皮。別咬這兩樣東西！

column1: The apple won't fit in the candy box.
column2: 蘋果塞不進糖果盒。

column1: The disco pass prefers saltier snacks.
column2: 迪斯可通行證更愛鹹一點的零食。

column1: There's no need to peel the apple.
column2: 沒必要削蘋果皮。

column1: Larry, it's pears in tequila bottles, not apples in wine boxes.
column2: Larry，那是龍舌蘭酒裡的梨，不是葡萄酒盒裡的蘋果。

column1: Seeing the melons on the front of that magazine, the apple develops an inferiority complex.
column2: 看到雜誌封面上的哈密瓜，蘋果就產生了自卑感。

column1: Applesauce. Good idea.
column2: 蘋果醬。好主意。

column1: You try to core the apple with the pill bottle, but are unable to remove the child safety lid!
column2: 你試著用藥瓶挖蘋果心，但拿不下兒童防護蓋！

column1: Tie a pink ribbon round your old apple... Nah. That just doesn't make it, Larry.
column2: 在你的老蘋果上綁條粉紅絲帶… 不。那根本不行，Larry。

column1: The apple is shocked and confused by the strange graffiti.
column2: 蘋果被奇怪的塗鴉嚇得懵懵懂懂。

column1: The ring might puncture the thin vinylette of your El Cheapo wallet.
column2: The ring might puncture the thin vinylette of your El Cheapo wallet.

column1: The breathspray looks at the ring in surprise! "Why, this is so sudden!"
column2: 口氣噴霧驚訝地看著戒指！"啊呀，這太突然了！"

column1: If clocks chime, do watches "ring?"
column2: 如果時鐘會敲鐘，那手錶會「發出鈴聲」嗎？

column1: Putting the ring in the apple might spoil its lustrous shine! (The apple's, that is!)
column2: 把戒指放進蘋果可能會毀掉它的光澤！（是蘋果的，那就是！）

column1: When you gaze deep into the diamond, you seem to hear the sound of wedding bells, and maybe diesel trucks.
column2: 當你凝視著鑽石深處，你彷彿聽到婚禮鐘聲，也許還有柴油卡車。

column1: Checking for dissolvable solids, you dip the ring into the whiskey. Nothing happens.
column2: 為了檢查是否有可溶固體，你把戒指浸進威士忌。什麼都沒發生。

column1: Just for fun, you use the ring to press some buttons on the remote control.
column2: 只是好玩，你用戒指按遙控器的按鈕。

column1: It's no use, the rose is already engaged to some pansy.
column2: 沒用，玫瑰花已經訂婚給某個小屁孩。

column1: That's a creative way to give someone a ring.
column2: 那是個有創意的求婚方式。

column1: You smear chocolate all over the ring, then lick it all off. Was that fun or what?
column2: 你在戒指上塗滿巧克力，然後全部舔掉。那不是挺爽的嗎？

column1: The disco pass has no use for the ring.
column2: 迪斯可通行證對戒指沒轍。

column1: You rub the diamond on the blade of your pocket knife and sharpen it ever so slightly.
column2: 你用鑽石摩擦小刀的刀刃，磨得越來越尖。

column1: Using the diamond, you scratch your initials into the cardboard box of wine.
column2: 用鑽石，你在紙箱酒盒上刻下自己的縮寫。

column1: These are only photographs of girls, Larry. None of them will marry you.
column2: 這些只是女孩的照片，Larry。她們都不會嫁給你。

column1: The ring won't fit on the hammer.
column2: 戒指套不進錘子。

column1: Don't put the ring in with the pills. You might accidentally swallow it.
column2: 別把戒指跟藥一起放。你可能會不小心吞了它。

column1: Your ribbon WOULD look sweet with a cute little bow, but you decide to save the ribbon to use elsewhere.
column2: 你的絲帶配個可愛小蝴蝶結確實會很漂亮，但你決定留著別的地方用。

column1: The ring read the graffiti while it was sitting on the sink.
column2: 戒指坐在洗手槽上時讀了那塗鴉。

column1: Your wallet is stained enough already.
column2: 你的錢包已經夠髒的了。

column1: You don't really want "Morning in the Distillery" breath?
column2: 你不想要"蒸餾廠早晨"口氣？

column1: You drop a little whiskey on your watch, just to clean the crystal.
column2: 你滴點威士忌在手錶上，就當清潔水晶。

column1: Is whiskey and apples an old family recipe?
column2: 威士忌配蘋果是你家的老食譜？

column1: You dribble a bit of whiskey onto the ring, hoping to kill the germs from the bathroom sink.
column2: 你滴點威士忌在戒指上，希望能殺死浴室水槽的細菌。

column1: There is a strong odor of petroleum by-products coming from this glass.
column2: 這杯子裡散發著濃烈的石油副產品味道。

column1: I'll bet a little whiskey would make that remote control more powerful, you conjecture mistakenly.
column2: 我打賭一點威士忌能讓那遙控器更強勁，你錯誤地推測。

column1: If the rose isn't already dead, that rotgut will put it out of its misery.
column2: 如果玫瑰還活著，那劣質酒會結束它的痛苦。

column1: Nobody's going to want to drink the whiskey after you've poured it in THERE!
column2: 你倒進去以後，沒人會想喝那威士忌！

column1: The candy is already well-soaked.
column2: 糖果已經夠浸濕的了。

column1: You dribble a little whiskey on the pass to remove that "never used" sheen.
column2: 你滴點威士忌在通行證上，想去掉那"從未使用"的光澤。

column1: Don't you know? Whiskey and weapons don't mix!
column2: 你不知道嗎？威士忌和武器不搭！

column1: Yes, a good malt Chablis is tough to find.
column2: 是啊，好的麥芽夏布利很難找。

column1: That will only make the pages even more sticky.
column2: 那只會讓紙頁黏得更厲害。

column1: The whiskey is all you need to get hammered!
column2: 威士忌就是你需要的全部東西才能醉倒！

column1: A Mickey Finn will not get you anywhere.
column2: 迷幻藥滴不了事。

column1: Great, now you've got a whiskey-soiled ribbon!
column2: 好了，現在你有條威士忌污漬的絲帶了！

column1: You slop a little whiskey onto your carefully-scrawled graffiti.
column2: 你濺點威士忌在你精心塗寫的塗鴉上。

column1: Infrared doesn't work on leather.
column2: 紅外線對皮革沒效。

column1: You try to change spray channels with the remote control, but it doesn't work.
column2: 你試著用遙控器換噴霧頻道，但沒用。

column1: You are surprised to see the watch change to another time zone, then revert back to local, Lost Wages time again!
column2: 你驚訝地看著手錶換到另一個時區，然後又改回 Lost Wages 當地時間！

column1: You attempt to change the apple into something else with the remote control, but nothing happens.
column2: 你試著用遙控器把蘋果變成別的東西，但什麼都沒發生。

column1: The remote control causes no change in the ring.
column2: 遙控器對戒指沒任何作用。

column1: You DO wish the whiskey would change to a better brand!
